title: Anchored random clusters and SLE excursions
authors: Federico Camia, Valentino F. Foit, Rongvoram Nivesvivat
abstract: We provide a pedagogical review of CFT techniques to compute certain Schramm-Loewner Evolution (SLE) observables in the upper half-plane. The approach relies on the ability to express the observables as bulk-boundary correlation functions that involve degenerate boundary operators and, therefore, obey certain differential equations. In particular, we recover Schramm's left-passage probability for SLE, the SLE Green's functions, and the generalized densities of ``anchored'' critical percolation clusters first obtained by Kleban, Simmons, and Ziff. We also obtain new formulas corresponding to the densities of pivotal points between critical Fortuin-Kasteleyn (FK) clusters.
summary: This paper provides a comprehensive review and extension of CFT methods to calculate geometric properties of critical systems, such as SLE curves and FK clusters. It successfully derives new analytical formulas for cluster densities and pivotal points by solving differential equations derived from conformal symmetry. The work unifies several previously separate results in the literature into a single framework.
main_problem: The paper aims to provide a pedagogical review and systematic extension of Conformal Field Theory (CFT) techniques to compute Schramm-Loewner Evolution (SLE) observables and the densities of anchored critical clusters in the upper half-plane.
main_result: The authors derive explicit analytical expressions for various cluster densities and SLE excursions, and calculate exact values for structure constants governing the merging of SLE curves and bubbles.
method: The study utilizes Conformal Field Theory (CFT) techniques, specifically solving Belavin-Polyakov-Zamolodchikov (BPZ) differential equations and applying the Coulomb gas formalism to represent physical observables as correlation functions.
model_or_system: The models studied include the Fortuin-Kasteleyn (FK) random cluster model, O(n) loop models, and Schramm-Loewner Evolution (SLE) in the critical regime.
key_observables: Anchored cluster densities, Schramm's left-passage probability, SLE Green's functions, densities of pivotal points, and structure constants for boundary operators.
important_parameters: The SLE parameter kappa, loop fugacity n, cluster weight Q, central charge c, and the cross-ratio xi.
main_assumptions: The approach assumes the existence of full 2D conformal symmetry in the scaling limit and relies on the use of degenerate boundary operators.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate FK clusters and their boundaries, the difference between percolation clusters and their hulls, the insertion of boundary and bulk operators, and contour plots of cluster densities.
paper_structure: The paper begins with a review of CFT techniques for SLE observables, moves to the application of BPZ equations for various cluster densities, extends the scope to the general FK model, and concludes with the derivation of structure constants and new predictions for cluster boundaries.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1: Example of FK clusters in black and their boundaries (loops) in green.
Figure 2: A percolation cluster in the upper half-plane touching the real line (a) and its hull (b).
Figure 5: A SLE curve starts and ends on the real line at x1 and x2, respectively. This corresponds to the insertion of the boundary 1-leg operators ϕ1.
Figure 6: A SLE curve starts and ends on the real line at x1 and x2, respectively, while passing through the point z.
Figure 7: SLE path touching the boundary, corresponding to the insertion of two bound- ary 2-leg operators and one spin field in the bulk.
